summary:
## Metadata
- File path: .github/workflows/diffdoc-summarize.yml
- File name: diffdoc-summarize.yml
- Extension: .yml
- Inferred language/type: GitHub Actions workflow YAML
- Content hash: 6f883bd5ead30f4099154e2bdd439a37
- Line count: 69
- Byte size: 2274
- Summary format: structured-functional-v1
- Notable symbols/classes/functions: workflow `DiffDoc Summaries`; jobs `summarize`; steps `Checkout repository`, `Setup Node.js`, `Install dependencies`, `Build DiffDoc`, `Update summary manifest`, `Commit manifest changes`
- External dependencies: GitHub Actions `actions/checkout@v4`, `actions/setup-node@v4`, Node.js 22, npm, repository build script `npm run build`, runtime command `node dist/index.js summarize`
- Internal dependencies: `.diffdoc/manifest.json`, `.diffdoc/summaries/**`, `.diffdoc/vectra/**`, built artifact `dist/index.js`
- Public API/exports: None identified.

## Purpose
This workflow automates DiffDoc summary generation on pushes to the `main` branch. It builds the project, runs the DiffDoc summarization command, and commits updated DiffDoc artifacts back to the repository when changes are produced.

## User-Visible Behavior
On qualifying pushes to `main`, GitHub Actions runs a job named `Update DiffDoc manifest`. The workflow checks out the repository, installs dependencies, builds the project, and executes the DiffDoc summarize command. If `.diffdoc/manifest.json` or files under `.diffdoc/summaries` change, the workflow commits those changes with a bot identity and pushes them to the branch. If no relevant artifact changes exist, it logs `No DiffDoc artifact changes to commit.` and exits without creating a commit.

## Business Rules
- The workflow triggers only on `push` events to the `main` branch.
- Changes to `.diffdoc/manifest.json`, `.diffdoc/summaries/**`, and `.diffdoc/vectra/**` do not trigger the workflow.
- The job runs with `contents: write` permission so it can commit and push updates.
- Only one run is grouped under `diffdoc-summarize-main` at a time; new runs do not cancel in-progress runs.
- Environment values are resolved from repository variables first, then secrets, with defaults for several settings.
- The DiffDoc summarize command runs in delta mode with summarize concurrency set to `2`.
- A commit is created only when `git status` shows changes in `.diffdoc/manifest.json` or `.diffdoc/summaries`.

## Data Inputs And Outputs
Inputs:
- GitHub push event on `main`
- Repository contents
- Repository variables and secrets:
  - `AI_PROVIDER`
  - `DIFFDOC_BASE_DIR`
  - `LOCAL_LLM_ENDPOINT`
  - `LOCAL_CHAT_MODEL`
  - `LOCAL_EMBED_ENDPOINT`
  - `LOCAL_EMBED_MODEL`
  - `CLOUD_LLM_ENDPOINT`
  - `CLOUD_CHAT_MODEL`
  - `CLOUD_EMBED_MODEL`
  - `OPENAI_API_KEY`
- npm dependencies installed via `npm ci`
- Built project output used by `node dist/index.js`

Outputs:
- Updated `.diffdoc/manifest.json`
- Updated files under `.diffdoc/summaries`
- Git commit and push back to the repository when changes exist
- Workflow log messages indicating whether changes were committed

## Side Effects
- Checks out repository history with full depth (`fetch-depth: 0`).
- Installs project dependencies.
- Builds the project artifacts.
- Runs the DiffDoc summarization command, which updates DiffDoc artifacts.
- Configures Git author identity for the commit.
- Creates and pushes a commit named `Update DiffDoc summaries` when artifact changes are detected.

## Error And Edge Cases
- If `npm ci`, `npm run build`, or `node dist/index.js summarize` fails, the workflow job fails.
- If no tracked DiffDoc artifact changes are present, the commit step exits successfully without committing.
- The commit step only checks `.diffdoc/manifest.json` and `.diffdoc/summaries`; changes elsewhere are ignored for commit decisions.
- Missing or invalid environment values for the selected AI provider may cause the summarize command to fail.
- Because concurrency does not cancel in-progress runs, multiple queued runs may complete sequentially.

## Dependencies
- GitHub Actions workflow engine
- `actions/checkout@v4`
- `actions/setup-node@v4`
- Node.js 22
- npm
- Project build script exposed by `package.json`
- Built CLI entrypoint at `dist/index.js`
- Git command-line tooling available on the runner
- Repository variables and secrets for AI/provider configuration
- OpenAI-compatible or local LLM/embedding endpoints, depending on configuration

## Operational Notes
- The workflow is intended for automated regeneration of DiffDoc artifacts on `main`.
- `fetch-depth: 0` is required so the job can commit and push changes cleanly.
- The commit logic is idempotent with respect to unchanged DiffDoc artifacts.
- The workflow assumes the build step produces `dist/index.js` before summarization runs.
- Provider selection and endpoint/model configuration are externalized through environment variables, making runtime behavior dependent on repository settings.
- The summarize command uses concurrency `2`, which may matter for performance and external API rate limits.
